problem.title: Majority Element II
problem.description: Given an integer array of size n, find all elements that appear more than n/3 times. There can be at most two such elements. Return all such elements in any order.
assumptions: There can be at most two majority elements (more than n/3 times each).
Try to solve in O(n) time and O(1) space.

example.explanation: 3 appears 2 times > 3/3 = 1. Extended Boyer-Moore: maintain two candidates and two counts. After a pass, verify both candidates actually exceed n/3.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Boyer-Moore Voting (Extended) [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Maintain two candidate/count pairs. For each number: if it matches a candidate, increment that count; else if a count is zero, adopt the number as a new candidate; else decrement both counts. After the pass, count actual occurrences of both candidates and include those that exceed n/3. At most two elements can pass the n/3 threshold.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List

def majority_element(nums: List[int]) -> List[int]:
    cand1, cnt1, cand2, cnt2 = 0, 0, 1, 0
    for n in nums:
        if n == cand1: cnt1 += 1
        elif n == cand2: cnt2 += 1
        elif cnt1 == 0: cand1, cnt1 = n, 1
        elif cnt2 == 0: cand2, cnt2 = n, 1
        else: cnt1 -= 1; cnt2 -= 1
    
    return [c for c in (cand1, cand2) if nums.count(c) > len(nums) // 3]
